highlights.text: Friday morning, we lost it, triggering the bear case, and down we went 100 points going elevator down .
I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow: Begins below 6623.” We then sold to 6524 low of day Friday bounced into the close Friday.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can see 6591, dip then tackle 6623.
Elevator down refers to a period of relentless selling where price loses every support for a few hours.
Short squeezes only occur when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown and this happens when price recovers a major nearby low.

Therefore the sequence is as follows: Price loses a shelf, goes elevator down , then short squeezes on a Failed Breakdown, and the short squeeze typically back-tests the shelf that caused the sell originally.
This then setup the next building block: A quality Failed Breakdown and short squeeze.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “This is where I get interested, and an obvious trade is the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6524 daily low.
This is a clear daily low and a flush and recovery of this is actionable.” Right off Sunday’s open, we swept 6524 down to 6505, recovered, and rallied to 6560 (Failed Breakdown).
Regardless of the tweets though, the task for bulls this week was to run with this Failed Breakdown and ultimately test then recover 6623.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: Massive Squeeze For SPX. Dead Cat Bounce, Or Is The Bottom In? March 24 Plan
